subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Standard game size** — The rulebook states standard play needs **at least 4 players**.
- **2-player variant** — A specific **2-player game variant** exists on the back page.
- **3-player variant** — A specific **3-player variant** exists on the back page.

#### Core Cards
- **Codename cards** — 200 total.
- **Key cards** — 40 total.

#### Identity/Cover Cards
- **Red agents** — 8 cards.
- **Blue agents** — 8 cards.
- **Double agent** — 1 card.
- **Innocent bystanders** — 7 cards.
- **Assassin** — 1 card.

#### Table Tools
- **Card stand** — 1 stand for the key card.
- **Sand timer** — 1 timer for optional time pressure.
- **Rulebook** — 1 rulebook.

#### In-Play Count Snapshot
- **Grid size** — Exactly 25 codenames are used each game (5x5).
- **Identity coverage** — 9 cards for the starting team, 8 for the other team, plus 7 bystanders and 1 assassin = 25 total identities.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Shuffle words** — Shuffle codename cards thoroughly; occasionally flip half the deck while shuffling.
2. **Build the grid** — Randomly choose 25 codenames and place them in a 5x5 layout.
3. **Prepare key** — Randomly choose 1 key card and slide it into the stand with either side up.
4. **Hide key** — Only spymasters may see the key card.
5. **Place team stacks** — Put red and blue agent stacks near their matching spymasters.
6. **Place neutral/danger stack** — Put innocent bystanders and assassin between spymasters.
7. **Set starting color** — Read the 4 edge lights on the key and set the double agent to that color.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 4+ Players (Standard) ---
- **Layout** — Use the full setup sequence above.

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **Rule location** — Use back-page 2-player setup/rules from the rulebook.

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Rule location** — Use back-page 3-player setup/rules from the rulebook.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Role Assignment
- **Teams** — Split into two teams with similar size and skill.
- **Spymasters** — Each team chooses exactly 1 spymaster.
- **Operatives** — All other players are field operatives.

#### Seating
- **Spymaster side** — Both spymasters sit on the same side of the table.
- **Operative side** — Operatives sit opposite their own spymaster.

#### Information Access
- **Spymasters** — May see the key card.
- **Operatives** — Must not see the key card.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 4+ Players (Standard) ---
- **Recommended baseline** — Two teams of two is the default minimum standard setup.

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **Rule location** — Use back-page 2-player role/setup adjustments.

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Rule location** — Use back-page 3-player role/setup adjustments.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Pre-First-Turn Checks
- **Starting team indicator** — Use the 4 edge lights on the key card.
- **Word target counts** — Starting team must find 9 words; other team must find 8 words.
- **Double agent assignment** — Double agent belongs to the starting team for the whole game.

#### First Action
1. **First clue** — The starting team's spymaster gives the first clue (one word + one number).

#### Player-Count Header
--- 4+ Players (Standard) ---
- **Start flow** — Use 9-vs-8 target structure and normal alternating turns.

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **Rule location** — Use back-page 2-player start adjustments.

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Rule location** — Use back-page 3-player start adjustments.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Team Turn Sequence
1. **Give clue** — Active spymaster says exactly one clue word and one number.
2. **Discuss** — Operatives may discuss openly while spymaster stays neutral.
3. **Make guess** — One operative touches one codename for the official guess.
4. **Reveal identity** — Spymaster covers that word with the correct identity card.
5. **Continue or stop** — If correct color, operatives may guess again or stop voluntarily.
6. **End turn** — Turn ends immediately on wrong guess, on voluntary stop, or at guess limit.
7. **Pass play** — Other team becomes active and gives next clue.

#### Guess-Limit Rule
- **Minimum guesses** — Operatives must make at least 1 guess each turn.
- **Maximum guesses** — Operatives may guess up to (clue number + 1).

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Spymaster Action
- **Allowed format** — Give exactly one word and one number.
- **Meaning rule** — Clue must connect by meaning, not by letter position or spelling tricks.
- **Visible-word ban** — Clue cannot be any visible codename on the table.
- **Word-form ban** — Clue cannot be a form/part of a visible codename (e.g., BREAK blocks break/broken/breakdown).
- **Compound-part ban** — Clue cannot be part of a visible compound codename.

#### Operative Actions
- **Discuss** — Operatives may debate before each official guess.
- **Guess** — Official guess is made by touching one codename.
- **Stop option** — Team may stop guessing voluntarily after at least one guess.
- **Extra-guess option** — Team may use one extra guess beyond the clue number.

#### Guess Outcomes
- **Own team word** — Cover with your team color; guessing may continue.
- **Innocent bystander** — Cover with bystander card; turn ends immediately.
- **Opponent word** — Cover with opponent color; turn ends and helps opponent.
- **Assassin** — Cover with assassin; game ends and guessing team loses immediately.

#### Invalid Clue Penalty
- **Immediate effect** — Clue-giving team's turn ends immediately.
- **Extra penalty** — Opposing spymaster may pre-cover one of their own words before next clue.
- **Missed challenge** — If no one notices invalidity in time, clue remains valid.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Communication Discipline
- **Spymaster posture** — Keep a straight face and avoid nonverbal signals.
- **Card-touch timing** — Spymaster should not reach toward cards while teammates are deciding.
- **Operative focus** — Operatives should avoid eye contact with spymaster while guessing.

#### Clue-Language Standards
- **Language** — Clues are in English.
- **Foreign word allowance** — A foreign word is allowed only if your group uses it in English speech.
- **Homonym rule** — Same sound + different spelling/meaning counts as different words.
- **Same-spelling rule** — Same spelling may be used even if pronunciation/meaning differs.
- **Spelling allowance** — Spelling out a clue is allowed for disambiguation.

#### Flexible Group Rules (Optional)
- **Abbreviations/acronyms** — Groups may allow common forms (e.g., CIA/UK/lol/PhD).
- **Proper names** — Groups may allow as long as other clue rules are respected.
- **Compound words** — Groups may allow broader compound handling by agreement.
- **Rhymes** — Groups may allow stricter or looser rhyme standards by agreement.

#### Expert Number Options
- **Zero clue** — Number 0 is allowed as an expert option.
- **Unlimited clue** — "Unlimited" is allowed as an expert option.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win/Loss Triggers
- **Primary win** — First team to cover all its own words wins immediately.
- **Cross-turn win** — You can win on the opponent's turn if they reveal your final word.
- **Instant loss** — Team that reveals the assassin loses immediately.

#### Scoring Model
- **Victory points** — Standard Codenames has no VP system.
- **Score sources** — There are no additive point sources in standard play.
- **Tiebreakers** — No tiebreak system is needed because outcome is immediate win/loss by trigger.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Spymaster Priorities
- **Safe multi first** — Open with a high-confidence 2-word clue instead of forcing a risky 4-word clue.
- **Assassin check** — Before speaking, test your clue against assassin associations first.
- **Ambiguity filter** — Reject clues that also strongly match enemy words.

#### Operative Priorities
- **Best-first ordering** — Guess your strongest match first to maximize chance of keeping turn alive.
- **Risk sort** — If two guesses are close, discuss order explicitly before touching any card.
- **Carryover memory** — Track unresolved past clues to use the +1 extra guess efficiently.

#### Team Calibration
- **Association style** — Align early on literal vs thematic interpretation.
- **Confidence language** — Mark guesses as high/medium/low confidence during discussion.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Greedy clue sizing** — Forcing oversized clues early increases assassin/enemy overlap risk.
- **Blind extra guess** — Taking the +1 guess without evidence often throws away tempo.
- **Legality miss** — Forgetting clue-form restrictions can cause immediate-turn loss and penalty.
- **Body-language dependence** — Reading facial cues breaks spirit and creates table disputes.
- **Static interpretation** — Reusing old assumptions after board changes causes avoidable wrong touches.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Clue Size
- **Conservative line** — Smaller clue reduces catastrophic error chance.
- **Aggressive line** — Bigger clue accelerates pace but raises assassin linkage risk.

#### Decision 2: Use of the +1 Guess
- **Push** — Use +1 when prior-clue carryover is strong and evidence-based.
- **Hold** — Decline +1 when confidence is weak to preserve next-turn board clarity.

#### Decision 3: Operative Stop Timing
- **Stop early** — End turn when remaining options are low-confidence.
- **Continue** — Keep guessing only when confidence remains materially higher than risk.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for scan-first readability with explicit player-count audit and player-count headers where variants are referenced in the rulebook text.
